task: Add a smaller, surgical "Fortress Mode" change that only implements a security-headers middleware and the status endpoint: create a new module (e.g., src/middleware/fortress.js) that sets standard security headers (X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block, Referrer-Policy: no-referrer, and an optional Strict-Transport-Security when FORTRESS_HSTS=true), export it for Express/Koa-style use, register it early in the main server startup file (src/server.js or app.js) so it applies to all routes, and add a simple GET /status/fo endpoint that returns 200 with JSON { status: "ok", fortress: true }; do not touch CORS, rate-limiting, or the plugin loader in this change so the edit stays minimal and easily revertible, and include a basic unit/integration test that the headers are present on a sample request.

subtasks.description: Create a new middleware module src/middleware/fortress.js that exports an Express middleware, a Koa middleware, and an Express Router with GET /status/fo which returns { status: 'ok', fortress: true }. The middleware will set the required security headers on responses and add Strict-Transport-Security when process.env.FORTRESS_HSTS === 'true'. The module should be safe to require (no server start) and designed for simple registration.
subtasks.acceptance_criteria: New file src/middleware/fortress.js exists, parses without runtime errors when required by node, and exports at least: expressMiddleware (function), koaMiddleware (function), and router (an Express Router).

changes.explanation: New middleware module implementing Express and Koa middlewares and an Express Router with GET /status/fo. Sets common security headers and conditionally adds HSTS when FORTRESS_HSTS==='true'. Safe to require without starting a server.

subtasks.description: Create a minimal Express startup file src/server.js that registers the fortress middleware early (app.use) so headers apply to all routes, mounts the fortress router (so GET /status/fo is available), and exports the Express app object without auto-listening when required (listen only if run directly). This keeps registration explicit and isolated to one new file.
subtasks.test_command: node -e "const app=require('./src/server.js'); if(!app || typeof app !== 'function'){console.error('app not exported'); process.exit(2);} console.log('ok')"
subtasks.acceptance_criteria: New file src/server.js exists, requiring it returns an Express app object (callable) and it registers the fortress middleware and the /status/fo route without starting a listening server when required.

subtasks.description: Add a simple integration test test/test_fortress.test.js that uses supertest to require the exported app from src/server.js and verifies: GET /status/fo returns 200 with JSON { status: 'ok', fortress: true } and the response contains the security headers: X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, X-XSS-Protection, Referrer-Policy. The test should also assert Strict-Transport-Security is present when process.env.FORTRESS_HSTS is 'true'.
subtasks.acceptance_criteria: Running 'node test/test_fortress.test.js' exits 0 and prints success messages (or nothing but exits 0). The test confirms that GET /status/fo returns 200 with the expected JSON body and that the required headers are present; when FORTRESS_HSTS=true, Strict-Transport-Security should be present.

plan_summary: Add a lightweight Node-style 'Fortress' middleware module that sets security headers (Express + Koa compatible), export a small router with GET /status/fo, register it by creating a minimal Express startup file that uses the middleware early, and add an automated test that checks the headers and /status/fo response. Keep the change small and isolated to new files so it's easy to revert.
risk_assessment: Medium -- adding Node files into a primarily Python/Flask repo risks confusing repository structure, but changes are isolated to new files and do not modify existing Python code. If a real Node server already exists, the created src/server.js may conflict; coder should check for an existing server.js/app.js and adapt if necessary.
